prompt: I want to run DSPy with a local model. I have Ollama installed with llama3.1. How do I connect DSPy to it?
expected_output: Configures dspy.LM with ollama_chat provider and sets num_ctx
assertions: Sets api_key='' (empty string, required by LiteLLM)
Sets num_ctx explicitly (not relying on Ollama 4096 default)
Calls dspy.configure(lm=lm) to set as default

prompt: My DSPy prompts are getting silently truncated when using Ollama. The outputs seem cut off and wrong. What is going on?
expected_output: Diagnoses the Ollama 4096 default context window issue
assertions: Identifies that Ollama defaults to 4096 token context window regardless of model capacity
Shows how to set num_ctx to a larger value (e.g., 8192 or 16384)
Mentions that DSPy prompts with few-shot demos can easily exceed 4096 tokens
Notes that larger num_ctx requires more VRAM and may cause OOM errors

prompt: I want to use different sized local models for different parts of my DSPy pipeline — a small fast model for classification and a bigger model for generation. How do I set this up with Ollama?
expected_output: Sets up multi-model pipeline with set_lm() per predictor
assertions: Creates two dspy.LM instances with different Ollama models (e.g., llama3.2:3b and llama3.1:8b)
Uses set_lm() to assign different models to different predictors
Sets appropriate num_ctx for each model size
Both LM instances point to the same Ollama api_base
